rosters.notes: Direct beneficiaries of AI data-centre power demand: Vertiv (cooling and power infrastructure), Eaton (power management systems), Quanta Services (grid and electrical engineering), Constellation Energy (nuclear power supply). XLU included as sector ETF proxy only.

AI semiconductor infrastructure: NVDA (GPU accelerators, AI infrastructure leadership), TSM (advanced foundry, N3/N2 capacity), AVGO (networking ASICs, AI cluster interconnects), AMD (GPU competition, CPU/EPYC), ASML (EUV lithography equipment, bottleneck for all advanced nodes). SMH as sector ETF proxy.

Net-interest-margin beneficiaries when rates rising and credit contained: JPMorgan (largest US bank, NIM leverage), Bank of America (most rate-sensitive of mega banks, high deposit beta), Wells Fargo (pure-play NIM expansion story), Goldman Sachs (capital markets + NIM). XLF as diversified financial sector proxy. Route bias swing_only: conditional tailwind only, not structural position candidates.

Oil supply shock beneficiaries: ExxonMobil (integrated oil, largest US energy co), Chevron (integrated oil, Permian exposure), Occidental Petroleum (pure-play E&P, high oil beta), SLB (oilfield services, rig count / capex spend beneficiary). XLE as energy sector ETF proxy. Route bias swing_or_intraday: supply shocks are often short-duration events.

Geopolitical risk prime contractors: Lockheed Martin (F-35, missile systems, largest US defence prime), Northrop Grumman (stealth bombers, space systems, ICBM modernisation), RTX (missiles, engines, radar — formerly Raytheon/United Technologies), General Dynamics (combat vehicles, submarines, Gulfstream). ITA as aerospace/defence ETF proxy.

High-quality compounders with strong free cash flow, minimal debt dependency, and resilient earnings: Microsoft (software monopoly, cloud, high FCF), Apple (brand loyalty, services, large cash), Costco (resilient consumer demand, minimal leverage), Berkshire (diversified quality with large cash). QUAL ETF (iShares MSCI USA Quality Factor) as sector proxy.

Defensive quality names with low volatility and resilient demand: Costco (essential retail), J&J (healthcare, stable cash flows), P&G (consumer staples, pricing power), Coca-Cola, PepsiCo. ETF proxies: XLP (Consumer Staples), XLV (Healthcare), SPLV (S&P 500 Low Volatility).

IWM (iShares Russell 2000 ETF) as small-cap proxy. Used for headwind monitoring only under credit stress — watchlist-only, non-executable. No core symbols — individual small-cap selection requires additional intelligence layer not yet built.

Approved memory/storage names with recurring advisory gap evidence. SNDK (SanDisk) and WDC (Western Digital) approved via coverage_gap_review: 22 occurrences each as advisory_unresolved current candidates. MU already covered under semiconductors. STX (Seagate Technology) approved: enterprise storage beneficiary with strong data-centre demand exposure.

Approved AI compute infrastructure names. IREN approved with caution via coverage_gap_review: power-constrained neocloud/compute operator with speculative risk profile. SMCI (Super Micro Computer) added: high-density server and rack-scale solutions for AI data centres. DELL (Dell Technologies) added: server and storage infrastructure for enterprise AI workloads. Approval does not imply all neocloud, bitcoin-miner or power-compute names are approved.

SaaS and enterprise cloud platforms benefiting from falling yields and risk-on rotation. CRM (Salesforce), NOW (ServiceNow), ADBE (Adobe), DDOG (Datadog). IGV as iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF proxy. MSFT covered separately in quality_cash_flow where its defensive quality angle is primary.

Cybersecurity platform vendors benefiting from AI-driven expansion of attack surface and enterprise security spend. CRWD (CrowdStrike), PANW (Palo Alto Networks), FTNT (Fortinet), ZS (Zscaler). CIBR as First Trust NASDAQ Cybersecurity ETF proxy.

Mega-cap digital platforms with advertising leverage and content. GOOGL (Alphabet — search, cloud, YouTube), META (Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp ad platform), NFLX (streaming content, ad tier growing). XLC as Communication Services SPDR ETF proxy.

Consumer discretionary names benefiting from risk-on rotation and consumer confidence. AMZN (retail + AWS growth optionality), HD (home improvement, consumer spend), MCD (resilient fast food with consumer upswing), NKE (aspirational brand, consumer confidence proxy). XLY as Consumer Discretionary SPDR ETF proxy.

Travel and hospitality names for risk-on rotation. BKNG (Booking Holdings — online travel, strong FCF), MAR (Marriott — hotel operator, RevPAR leverage), DAL (Delta — airline, operational leverage), ABNB (Airbnb — platform model, asset-light). JETS as US Global Jets ETF proxy. Note: rule is blocked if oil_supply_shock fires — oil shock headwind takes precedence.

Large-cap defensive healthcare for risk-off rotation. UNH (UnitedHealth — managed care, resilient earnings), LLY (Eli Lilly — GLP-1/obesity franchise, earnings momentum), ABBV (AbbVie — diversified pharma, dividend), TMO (Thermo Fisher — healthcare infrastructure, defensive revenue). XLV as Health Care SPDR ETF proxy. Note: JNJ covered in defensive_quality.

Large-cap biotech for risk-on environments. REGN (Regeneron), VRTX (Vertex Pharmaceuticals — cystic fibrosis franchise), AMGN (Amgen — diversified large-cap biotech). XBI as SPDR S&P Biotech ETF proxy for broader biotech exposure. Swing only — binary catalyst risk means position sizing must be managed.

Regional bank names for credit easing and risk-on environments. RF (Regions Financial), CFG (Citizens Financial), FITB (Fifth Third Bancorp). KRE as SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF proxy. Swing only — regional banks are more volatile than large-cap and require credit quality confirmation.

Industrial infrastructure and reshoring beneficiaries. CAT (Caterpillar — heavy machinery, infrastructure spend), DE (Deere — agriculture and construction equipment), GE (GE Aerospace — jet engines, defence), URI (United Rentals — equipment rental, construction activity proxy). XLI as Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF proxy. Note: PWR and ETN covered in data_centre_power for the AI-specific electrical infrastructure angle.

Gold and precious metals for safe-haven bid and risk-off rotation. NEM (Newmont — largest gold miner), AEM (Agnico Eagle — diversified gold miner). GLD (SPDR Gold Shares) as direct gold price proxy ETF. GDX (VanEck Gold Miners ETF) as sector proxy. Note: GLD classified as etf_proxy to get appropriate confidence scaling.

Copper and electrification materials for risk-on and industrial demand. FCX (Freeport-McMoRan — largest US copper miner), SCCO (Southern Copper — pure-play copper with high dividend). XLB as Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF proxy for broader materials exposure.

REIT names for falling yield tailwind. PLD (Prologis — industrial REIT, logistics/e-commerce), AMT (American Tower — cell tower REIT, infrastructure), EQIX (Equinix — data centre REIT, AI infrastructure angle). XLRE as Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF proxy. Note: yields_rising_to_reits_headwind rule generates these as pressure_candidates when yields are rising.

Small-cap exposure for risk-on and credit-easing environments. IWM (iShares Russell 2000 ETF) and IJR (iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF) as liquid ETF proxies only. No individual small-cap selection — individual selection requires additional intelligence layer not yet built. Note: distinct from the existing small_caps headwind theme which fires under credit stress.
